title: narcissus hispanicus subsp. munozii-garmendiae
intro: Narcissus hispanicus subsp. munozii-garmendiae, also known as Munoz's narcissus or Spanish narcissus, is a perennial bulbous plant belonging to the Amaryllidaceae family. It is native to the Iberian Peninsula and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues and fever. The plant contains alkaloids such as galantamine, which has been found to have potential health benefits, including improving cognitive function and reducing anxiety. The plant is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including colds, flu, and insomnia.
